name: OSM proximity / POI layer (Overpass + OSMnx-features + Nominatim)
role: Answer the real geospatial questions: 'what's near (lat,lng)?', 'are these roads near schools?', 'what neighborhood is this?'. Feeds caution-zone weighting into the reward.
decision: Add this. It is the correct primitive for coordinate/radius proximity. Build school/POI masks over the road graph at init so the reward can weight caution near schools/crossings.
license: ODbL (OSM data); Overpass/Nominatim public servers free under usage policies
outputs: GeoDataFrame of POIs (schools, hospitals, crossings...) with geometry
road-near-X boolean masks via geopandas sjoin_nearest (reproject to metric CRS e.g. EPSG:3310)
how: OSMnx: ox.features_from_point((lat,lng), tags={'amenity':'school'}, dist=500)  -- NOTE dist is a square bbox half-width, not a circle; post-filter by geodesic distance or use Overpass around: for a true radius
Nominatim reverse geocode for neighborhood labels (max 1 req/sec, mandatory User-Agent)
alternatives: Google Places / Foursquare / Mapbox Search (API key + billing, richer but paid)
risks: Nominatim public server: 1 req/sec, no bulk -> cache
Overpass soft limits ~10k queries/day; set a User-Agent
reproject to a metric CRS before any distance math
